generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically retrieve and refresh all Event and Activity data from Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0 on a fixed 60-second cycle without requiring user intervention.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 mandates a 60-second automated refresh cycle as the primary mechanism for real-time situational awareness.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a Pause control that suspends the automatic 60-second data refresh cycle, and a Resume control that immediately restarts the 60-second cycle from the moment of resumption.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 explicitly permits manual pause and mandates cycle resumption on user command to support briefing moments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a visible staleness indicator showing the elapsed time since the last successful data refresh from each source system; if a refresh fails, the indicator shall clearly distinguish between stale data and a source system connectivity failure.
generated_requirements.rationale: Graceful degradation with a staleness indicator is critical so controllers are not misled by outdated data during high-tempo exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: When one or both source system integrations become unavailable mid-exercise, the system shall continue to display the last successfully retrieved dataset (last-known-good data) and shall present a prominently visible alert banner identifying which source system(s) are unreachable and the timestamp of the last successful retrieval in both local and exercise time.
generated_requirements.rationale: Graceful degradation prevents a blank or crashed display during source system outages, preserving situational awareness with appropriate caveats.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement a Multi-Document Interface (MDI) layout that allocates exactly the left two-thirds of the application window to a graphical timeline view and exactly the right one-third to a data table region; this ratio shall be fixed and not user-configurable.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-02 mandates a fixed 2:1 screen split to ensure consistent projection-room situational awareness layout.

generated_requirements.text: Within the data table region, the top one-quarter shall display sequential Events (order-dependent, not time-bound) and the bottom three-quarters shall display temporally relevant Events ordered by their proximity to current exercise time; this segmentation shall be derived from a field or attribute present in the MSEL source data and shall not be computed by EEMDS.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-03 and A-08 define the table segmentation logic and its authoritative source to prevent misclassification by the display layer.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall color-code all displayed Events and Activities based on their variance from current exercise time using the following threshold bands: (a) On-Time — variance within ±[N] minutes, displayed using DHS-approved color code [TBD per DHS color schema]; (b) At-Risk — variance between ±[N+1] and ±[M] minutes, displayed using DHS-approved color code [TBD]; (c) Overdue/Early — variance exceeding ±[M] minutes, displayed using DHS-approved color code [TBD]. Exact threshold values and color codes shall be defined during requirements elaboration using the DHS color schema specification document.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 requires color-coded variance indicators; explicit threshold bands and DHS color code references prevent ambiguous implementation decisions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display two independent, simultaneously visible clocks at all times: one showing current local (wall-clock) time and one showing current exercise time; the two clocks shall be visually distinct in label and position.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 mandates dual-clock display to eliminate time-reference confusion in exercises where exercise time diverges from wall-clock time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall obtain exercise time from a designated authoritative exercise clock service (to be specified during integration design); if the exercise clock service is unreachable, the system shall display a clearly labeled warning that exercise time is unavailable and shall not substitute local time as a proxy for exercise time.
generated_requirements.rationale: Specifying the authoritative time source and failure behavior prevents silent time-reference errors that would corrupt schedule-variance calculations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to define, name, and configure time segments used to organize Events on the graphical timeline; time segments shall be configurable per exercise and may be created or modified prior to or during exercise execution.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 requires user-defined time segments to support exercise-specific timeline organization.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support filtering of displayed Events and Activities by (a) Venue and (b) Key Events designation; filters shall be applicable independently or in combination, and the active filter state shall be visibly indicated in the display at all times.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-11 mandates multi-dimensional filtering to reduce cognitive load during fast-moving exercise scenarios.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an on-demand 'Full Exercise View' control that clears all active filters and displays all Events and Activities in the exercise without requiring the user to manually reset each filter.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-13 requires an unfiltered view accessible on demand to ensure no Events are inadvertently hidden from controllers.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display only MSEL Summary data fields in both the graphical timeline nodes and the data table rows; full MSEL detail records shall not be rendered within EEMDS.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 restricts the display scope to MSEL Summary data to maintain display clarity and respect source system data boundaries.

generated_requirements.text: Each displayed Event and Activity shall include a direct deep-link control that, when activated, opens the corresponding record in the originating source application (Sync Matrix 1.0 or EMT 1.0); EEMDS shall not provide any mechanism to create, edit, or delete Event or Activity data directly.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 establishes EEMDS as a read-only display tool and requires deep-link navigation to source applications for all modifications.

generated_requirements.text: When a user activates a deep-link to a source application and that application is not running or not reachable on the local machine, the system shall display a descriptive error message identifying the unreachable application and providing guidance on how to launch or reconnect to it; the EEMDS display shall remain fully operational.
generated_requirements.rationale: Specifying deep-link failure behavior prevents silent failures that leave users without recourse during time-critical exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate and maintain a history report that captures all changes to Event and Activity data reflected in EEMDS, including: (a) the identity of the user who made the change in the source system, (b) the field(s) changed, (c) the previous value, (d) the new value, (e) the timestamp of the change in both local time and exercise time, and (f) the source application in which the change originated.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-12 and the AAR Analyst stakeholder need require a complete, field-level audit trail to support post-exercise after-action reconstruction.

generated_requirements.text: The history report shall be retrievable at any point during or after the exercise; it shall be exportable in at minimum CSV and PDF formats; export shall be executable without interrupting the live display or data refresh cycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: AAR Analysts and Exercise Directors require on-demand, non-disruptive access to the audit log in portable formats for reporting and analysis.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate actual-versus-assumed time variance charts for Events and Activities, displaying the quantitative difference between the planned (assumed) time and the actual execution time for each Event or Activity.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD Future State and Scope sections identify variance charts as a required analytical output for schedule adherence assessment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control with at minimum the following roles: Exercise Controller, Exercise Director/Senior Observer, Exercise Planner, AAR Analyst, and IT Administrator; each role shall have defined permissions governing access to display views, filter configuration, time segment configuration, history report retrieval, and user management functions.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 and the stakeholder matrix require role differentiation to ensure appropriate access boundaries even within the read-only display context.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate every user prior to granting access using the DHS-standard authentication mechanism (CAC/PIV card authentication integrated with Active Directory); anonymous and guest access modes shall not be supported.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 and DHS IT security policy mandate pre-authorized user authentication; CAC/PIV is the DHS standard for internal operational tools.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall deny access and log the attempt for any user who fails authentication or is not present in the authorized user list; the denial event shall be recorded in the audit log with timestamp, attempted username, and workstation identifier.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 7 requires 100% enforcement of access control with zero unauthorized access incidents, necessitating logged denial events.

generated_requirements.text: Upon application crash or unplanned client PC reboot, the system shall automatically restore the last active filter state, time segment configuration, display layout, and pause/resume state upon next successful launch and authentication, without requiring manual reconfiguration by the user.
generated_requirements.rationale: Session state persistence prevents loss of critical display configuration during mid-exercise failures, minimizing recovery time in high-tempo scenarios.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a dedicated Projection Display Mode with increased font sizes, high-contrast color rendering, and reduced layout density optimized for legibility at distances up to 100 feet on a 10×10 projection screen, selectable independently from the standard operator workstation view.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objectives 2 and 8 and OC-03 require a projection-optimized mode distinct from close-range workstation use to meet readability KPIs at operational distances.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0 using their existing, documented data interfaces (APIs, database connections, or file exports) without requiring any modification to either source application.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-02 and the out-of-scope boundary prohibit modifications to source applications, requiring EEMDS to adapt to existing interfaces.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall lock to a specified version of each source application's data interface; if a source application interface version changes, the system shall detect the version mismatch, alert the IT Administrator, and continue operating on last-known-good data until the interface is validated and updated.
generated_requirements.rationale: Legacy interface stability risk (D-01, D-02) requires version locking and change detection to prevent silent data feed breakage.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display applicable data sensitivity or classification markings (e.g., FOUO) on all screens and exported reports in accordance with the exercise's applicable security classification level.
generated_requirements.rationale: MSEL data may carry FOUO or exercise-sensitive markings; federal data handling requirements mandate visible classification labeling on all displays and outputs.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete each 60-second data refresh cycle — including retrieval from both source systems, processing, and full display update — within 10 seconds of the scheduled refresh trigger, such that the display reflects current data for at least 50 of every 60 seconds.
generated_requirements.rationale: OC-02 requires the 60-second refresh cycle to complete without perceptible degradation; a 10-second processing budget ensures display currency is maintained.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall render any user-initiated display interaction (filter application, view switch, time segment selection, full exercise view) within 3 seconds on the target client PC hardware under normal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Rapid UI response is essential during high-tempo exercise execution where controller decision-making depends on immediate display feedback.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be available 99% of the time during defined normal business hours (0800–1800 local time, Monday through Friday, and during any scheduled exercise window); availability shall be measured as the proportion of scheduled operating minutes during which the application is successfully launchable and the display is rendering current or last-known-good data on the designated client PC.
generated_requirements.rationale: OC-01 and Business Objective 3 mandate 99% availability; the measurement definition reconciles the client-PC deployment model (TC-01) with the availability KPI.

generated_requirements.text: All planned maintenance activities shall be scheduled and executed outside of defined normal business hours and outside of any scheduled exercise window; the IT Administrator shall provide at least 48 hours advance notice to exercise stakeholders before any planned maintenance.
generated_requirements.rationale: OC-01 requires maintenance windows outside business hours to protect the 99% availability commitment during operational periods.

generated_requirements.text: All visual design elements — including status indicator colors, background colors, text colors, highlight colors, and chart colors — shall use only color values explicitly defined in the DHS color schema specification document; the specific approved color codes (hex or RGB values) shall be referenced by identifier in the UI design specification and shall not be substituted with visually similar but non-approved values.
generated_requirements.rationale: RC-01 and BR-05 prohibit any deviation from the DHS color schema; referencing specific color codes prevents implementation drift.

generated_requirements.text: In Projection Display Mode, all Event and Activity status color indicators shall be correctly identifiable by 90% of viewers at a viewing distance of 100 feet from a 10×10 projection screen; Event and Activity detail text shall be legible by 90% of viewers at a viewing distance of 30 feet from the same screen.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objectives 2 and 8 define specific distance-based readability KPIs that must be met for the projection operations center use case.

generated_requirements.text: 90% of new users assigned the Exercise Controller role shall be able to independently initiate an Event or Activity display, apply a filter, and navigate to a source application record within 90 minutes of first use, without formal training, as validated by usability testing.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 4 defines a measurable learnability KPI that must be validated through structured usability testing.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards for all operator workstation views, including sufficient color contrast ratios, keyboard navigability, and screen reader compatibility, while maintaining DHS color schema compliance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act requires federal government systems to meet WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility standards.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement security controls aligned to NIST SP 800-53 Revision 5 control families AC (Access Control), AU (Audit and Accountability), and IA (Identification and Authentication) at the impact level appropriate to the exercise classification, as determined by the DHS IT Security Officer.
generated_requirements.rationale: FISMA compliance requires DHS internal operational tools to implement documented NIST SP 800-53 controls across the relevant security control families.

generated_requirements.text: All data in transit between EEMDS and source systems (Sync Matrix 1.0 and EMT 1.0) shall be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher; data at rest in the local history report store shall be encrypted using AES-256 or an equivalent DHS-approved encryption standard.
generated_requirements.rationale: DHS IT security policy and FISMA require encryption of sensitive operational data both in transit and at rest.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce session timeout after a configurable period of inactivity (default: 15 minutes); upon timeout, the display shall lock and require re-authentication before resuming; the display shall remain visible in a locked state to preserve projection-room situational awareness.
generated_requirements.rationale: DHS IT security policy requires inactivity timeouts to prevent unauthorized access from unattended workstations while preserving the shared display function.

generated_requirements.text: The audit log shall be tamper-evident; any attempt to modify or delete history report records shall be detectable, and the system shall alert the IT Administrator upon detection of any such attempt.
generated_requirements.rationale: NIST SP 800-53 AU controls and the AAR integrity requirement demand that audit records be protected against unauthorized modification.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable as a standalone Windows client application on a standard DHS-issued PC without requiring server-side rendering infrastructure, cloud connectivity, or elevated administrator privileges beyond initial installation.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-01 mandates client-PC deployment with no server-side or cloud dependency to support operations center environments with constrained infrastructure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support deployment on any Windows 10 or Windows 11 client PC meeting the minimum hardware specification defined in the deployment guide, without requiring custom hardware or specialized graphics cards beyond standard DHS-issued workstation configurations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Portability across standard DHS-issued hardware ensures the system can be deployed in any exercise operations center without procurement of specialized equipment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall include an integration adapter layer that isolates source system interface logic from display logic; each source system adapter shall be independently updatable to accommodate interface version changes in Sync Matrix 1.0 or EMT 1.0 without requiring changes to the core display application.
generated_requirements.rationale: Legacy interface instability risk (D-01, D-02) requires an architecturally isolated adapter layer to minimize the cost and risk of interface updates.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an IT Administrator configuration interface for managing the authorized user list, role assignments, session timeout values, and source system connection parameters, without requiring code changes or application redeployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Operational flexibility requires that IT Administrators can manage access and configuration without developer involvement between exercises.

generated_requirements.text: The system codebase shall include inline documentation and a developer maintenance guide sufficient for a qualified Windows application developer unfamiliar with the original development team to diagnose and resolve common defects within one business day.
generated_requirements.rationale: Government systems frequently experience developer turnover; documented, maintainable code reduces long-term sustainment risk.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all integration errors, authentication failures, data refresh failures, and application exceptions to a local diagnostic log file; the log shall be retrievable by the IT Administrator without requiring access to the application's user interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: Diagnostic logging enables rapid root-cause analysis of failures during or after exercise execution without relying on user-reported symptoms.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a Mean Time To Recovery (MTTR) of no more than 30 minutes for any single-point failure on the client PC (e.g., application crash, OS restart), measured from the time of failure to the time the display is fully operational with restored session state.
generated_requirements.rationale: Rapid recovery from client-side failures is essential to maintain the 99% availability commitment and minimize disruption during live exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall not lose any history report records due to an application crash or unplanned reboot; the history log shall be persisted to durable local storage after each write operation using a write-ahead or equivalent journaling mechanism.
generated_requirements.rationale: Audit log completeness is a hard requirement for after-action review; data loss due to crashes would undermine the integrity of post-exercise analysis.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a documented regression test suite covering all source system integration interfaces; this suite shall be executed and pass before any deployment of an updated version of EEMDS or following any reported change to a source system interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: Interface regression testing is required to detect silent data feed breakage caused by source system updates before they affect live exercise operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall conform to all applicable DHS IT security policies and FISMA requirements as assessed through a formal Authority to Operate (ATO) process prior to first operational use; the system shall maintain ATO compliance through its operational lifecycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: All DHS internal operational tools require a formal ATO under FISMA before deployment; this requirement ensures the process is planned and tracked.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a change notification mechanism that alerts the IT Administrator via an in-application alert when a source system (Sync Matrix 1.0 or EMT 1.0) reports a data interface version change or schema modification, and shall log the notification with timestamp for traceability.
generated_requirements.rationale: Proactive interface change notification (D-01, D-02) enables the IT Administrator to initiate regression testing and adapter updates before silent data feed breakage affects exercise operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a user-accessible help reference within the application that describes the color-coding scheme (including variance thresholds and corresponding DHS color codes), filter controls, dual-clock interpretation, and deep-link navigation, accessible without leaving the display context.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 4 requires 90% of new users to operate independently within 90 minutes; embedded contextual help supports self-service learning without formal training.

sme_subject: DHS exercise management and operations center systems specialist
sme_advisory: EEMDS is a real-time situational awareness display tool operating within a DHS exercise operations center environment, where high-tempo, time-critical decision-making depends on display fidelity, data currency, and access integrity. The requirements engineer must pay close attention to DHS-specific regulatory standards (color schema, IT security policy, FISMA), the operational realities of projection-room use, and the strict read-only/display-only boundary between EEMDS and its authoritative source systems. Special care should be taken to define precise behavioral requirements around data staleness, graceful degradation when source systems are unavailable, and the completeness and retrievability of the audit/history report — all of which are common failure points in government exercise management systems that surface during after-action reviews.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require every user to authenticate with valid credentials (username and password) before granting access to any system function, and shall verify that the authenticated user holds active MLS membership status at the time of each login.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 and MLS governance rules mandate that only registered, MLS-member realtors may access any system function or MLS data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall revoke an authenticated session and deny further access within 15 minutes of detecting that a realtor's MLS membership status has lapsed or been suspended, sourcing membership status from the authoritative realtor registry.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS board rules require near-real-time enforcement of membership status to prevent unauthorized access to proprietary listing data.

generated_requirements.text: When a session token expires while the device is offline, the system shall permit continued read-only access to locally cached data for the duration of the current offline period, and shall require re-authentication upon the next successful network connection before permitting any write operations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Agents in the field must not be fully locked out of cached data due to token expiry during connectivity loss, but write access must remain protected.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authenticated realtors to search MLS property listings by specifying criteria including, but not limited to, location, price range, property type, number of bedrooms, and number of bathrooms, and shall display matching results including at least one property image per listing.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS search with image display is a core in-scope capability required to eliminate the field productivity gap identified in the problem statement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall return MLS property search results to the user within 30 seconds of query submission under normal operating conditions, for 100% of search requests.
generated_requirements.rationale: The 30-second search result ceiling is a hard performance constraint defined in the business objectives and technical constraints sections.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authenticated realtors to create new MLS property listings from the mobile device, enforcing client-side data validation rules that mirror the MLS board's required fields, permissible value ranges, and photo resolution and count minimums before submission.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-02 and MLS board rules require that only valid, well-formed listing data is submitted to the MLS to prevent silent submission failures.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authenticated realtors to update existing MLS property listings from the mobile device, applying the same client-side validation rules as listing creation before submitting changes to the MLS.
generated_requirements.rationale: In-scope listing management requires update capability with the same data integrity controls as creation per BR-02.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall create and maintain an immutable audit log for every MLS listing create and update operation, capturing the realtor's identity, timestamp, fields changed, before and after values, and the MLS submission outcome (success or failure with error detail).
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS board compliance and potential legal discovery obligations in real estate transaction disputes require a traceable change history for all listing modifications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow a realtor to save MLS property search results to local device storage for later review, and shall make saved results accessible in offline mode.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 requires that realtors can save and subsequently access search results without an active internet connection.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a maximum cache age for MLS listing data stored on the device, purging or flagging as stale any listing data that exceeds the MLS board's permitted cache window (not to exceed 72 hours), and shall not display purged listings in offline mode.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS IDX/VOW policy rules prohibit retaining and displaying listing data beyond defined freshness windows, including sold, expired, or withdrawn listings.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display MLS listing data in compliance with applicable MLS board IDX/VOW policy requirements, including mandatory attribution (listing broker/agent credit), data freshness indicators, and any prohibited-use restrictions on cached data.
generated_requirements.rationale: IDX and VOW policy compliance is a non-negotiable regulatory constraint governing how MLS data may be displayed on any platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system's MLS integration layer shall conform to the RESO Data Dictionary field naming conventions and shall support either the RETS protocol or the RESO Web API standard as exposed by the MLS provider, with field mappings validated against the MLS's published data dictionary prior to deployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: RESO/RETS standards govern MLS data exchange structure; non-conformance will cause integration failures and data integrity violations.

generated_requirements.text: For MLS listing images displayed on the device, the system shall download and cache thumbnail-resolution images by default, and shall download full-resolution images only on explicit user request, subject to available device storage.
generated_requirements.rationale: Windows CE and Palm OS devices have severely constrained memory and storage; thumbnail-first image handling prevents storage exhaustion and supports performance targets.

generated_requirements.text: When image assets for a listing are unavailable in offline mode, the system shall display a clearly labelled placeholder and shall not display an error that prevents the user from viewing the remaining listing data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Graceful degradation of image display ensures agents can still access critical property data when cached images are absent or purged.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate a Comparative Market Analysis (CMA) report based on property criteria explicitly specified by the realtor, and shall deliver the completed report to the user within 60 seconds of criteria submission.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 prohibits CMA generation without explicit criteria input, and the 60-second delivery ceiling is a hard performance constraint from the business objectives.

generated_requirements.text: Every generated CMA report shall include a data provenance section stating the date and time the comparable sales data was sourced, the MLS data source identifier, and a staleness warning if any comparable data exceeds 24 hours in age.
generated_requirements.rationale: Agents presenting CMAs to clients must be able to defend data currency; transparency about data age is required for professional and regulatory credibility.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow a realtor to initiate a CMA report directly from a selected MLS search result record, passing the property context (MLS ID, address, property attributes) to the CMA tool without requiring the realtor to re-enter property data.
generated_requirements.rationale: The common workflow of search → select property → run CMA requires seamless property context handoff between modules to eliminate re-entry friction.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a map showing the locations of properties in a specified area, and shall provide turn-by-turn driving directions from the realtor's current location to any property selected from MLS search results, using an integrated third-party mapping service.
generated_requirements.rationale: Integrated mapping and directions eliminates the need for separate navigation tools, directly addressing the navigation overhead pain point.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict the display of maps and driving directions exclusively to properties that exist within the MLS dataset accessible to the authenticated realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 mandates that mapping features are scoped to MLS-accessible properties only, enforcing data governance boundaries.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow a realtor to navigate directly to the mapping and directions feature from a selected MLS search result, passing the property address and MLS ID without requiring manual re-entry.
generated_requirements.rationale: The search → select property → get directions workflow requires property context handoff to the mapping module to reduce friction in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall download and display the authenticated realtor's client contacts and scheduled appointments, synchronized from the office system, and shall allow the realtor to view and manage these records from the mobile device.
generated_requirements.rationale: Contact and appointment management is a core in-scope capability required to eliminate data fragmentation and field dependency on office systems.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically synchronize contact and appointment data with the office system once every hour without requiring any action from the realtor, ensuring that locally stored data is never more than 60 minutes stale under normal connectivity conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-03 mandates hourly automatic synchronization to keep field data current and aligned with the office system.

generated_requirements.text: When an hourly synchronization cycle partially fails (e.g., contacts synchronize successfully but appointments do not), the system shall display a clear notification to the realtor identifying which data domains failed to sync, the time of the last successful sync for each domain, and the estimated staleness of the affected data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Agents must be informed of data staleness at a domain level to make informed decisions in the field; silent partial failures erode trust and can cause professional errors.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify the realtor immediately upon detecting, during an automatic synchronization cycle, that a new appointment has been added to their schedule in the office system.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 requires that realtors are notified of new appointments discovered during synchronization to eliminate scheduling lag.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall initiate outbound calls to sellers or buyers on behalf of the realtor for the purpose of scheduling appointments, and shall notify the realtor immediately when the called party responds to the appointment request.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 and BR-10 require automated appointment scheduling calls and immediate realtor notification upon client response.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict outbound appointment calls exclusively to contact phone numbers associated with the authenticated realtor's own client records, and shall maintain a traceable call log per call recording the realtor identity, contact identity, timestamp, call outcome, and appointment context.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 requires traceability of all system-initiated communications, and MLS accountability rules and potential legal discovery obligations require a durable call record.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce TCPA compliance for all outbound appointment calls, including verification that the called party has provided prior express consent, scrubbing the number against applicable do-not-call registries, and restricting call initiation to between 8:00 AM and 9:00 PM local time of the called party.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated outbound calls to consumers are subject to TCPA regulations; non-compliance exposes the brokerage to significant legal liability.

generated_requirements.text: When internet connectivity is unavailable, the system shall automatically enter offline mode and provide read-only access to all previously downloaded and cached data, including MLS search results, CMA reports, contacts, and appointments, without requiring any action from the realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 mandates seamless offline read access to cached data to eliminate the productivity gap caused by poor field connectivity.

generated_requirements.text: When the device is in offline mode, the system shall queue any write operations (including listing updates, contact modifications, and appointment changes) that cannot be completed without connectivity, and shall automatically submit the queued operations to the server upon reconnection.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 requires that offline write operations are not lost but are deferred and submitted upon reconnection to maintain data integrity.

generated_requirements.text: Upon reconnection after an offline period, if a queued offline write operation conflicts with a server-side change to the same record made during the offline period, the system shall apply a defined conflict resolution policy (last-write-wins by server timestamp as default), notify the realtor of each conflict detected, display the conflicting values, and allow the realtor to manually override the resolution for each conflict.
generated_requirements.rationale: Offline write queues create merge conflict risk; a defined and user-visible conflict resolution strategy is essential to prevent silent data loss.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall save and restore all user preferences — including search filter settings, display settings, and saved search results — across application sessions and following any system failure or unplanned restart, with 100% of saved preferences restored to their last known state upon recovery.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 mandates complete preference persistence and restoration to protect data integrity and eliminate re-configuration overhead after failures.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall continuously monitor available local device storage and shall warn the realtor with a non-dismissible alert when available storage falls below a defined threshold (to be determined during design based on target device capacity), before any caching operation that would exhaust remaining storage.
generated_requirements.rationale: Palm OS and Windows CE devices have severely limited flash storage; proactive storage warnings prevent silent cache failures and data loss.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a manual cache-clearing mechanism that allows the realtor to selectively delete cached MLS search results, images, CMA reports, or all cached data from the device to reclaim storage space.
generated_requirements.rationale: Agents on resource-constrained devices must have direct control over local storage to manage device capacity without requiring IT intervention.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall be installable by an untrained realtor on a Windows CE or Palm OS device without reference to separately printed instructions, and at least 90% of untrained realtors must successfully complete self-installation in a usability evaluation.
generated_requirements.rationale: The 90% self-installation success rate for untrained users is a measurable business objective and operational constraint that directly enables rapid agent onboarding.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall conform to the native UI standards and conventions of the target platform (Windows CE or Palm OS respectively), using standard platform menus, navigation buttons, and interaction patterns, and shall use terminology natural to the real estate profession throughout.
generated_requirements.rationale: Platform-native UI conventions and domain-appropriate terminology are required to achieve zero-training usability and meet the self-service installation constraint.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be usable by a realtor with no formal training on the product, such that a first-time user can locate and execute core functions (MLS search, CMA generation, contact lookup, appointment view, and driving directions) without assistance.
generated_requirements.rationale: The no-training-required constraint is a core business objective; the UI must be self-explanatory for all primary workflows.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum availability of 99.9% measured across all access attempts on a 24/7/365 basis, permitting no more than 1 failed access attempt per 1,000 attempts.
generated_requirements.rationale: The 99.9% availability target is a hard business objective required to support agents operating at all hours in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system backend shall support a minimum of 1,000 simultaneous authenticated users at launch, and the architecture shall be designed to scale to support 5,000 simultaneous users within 12 months of launch without requiring a system redesign.
generated_requirements.rationale: The scalability thresholds of 1,000 at launch and 5,000 within 12 months are explicit business objectives driven by the anticipated fivefold agent growth.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between the mobile application and the backend servers (including authentication credentials, MLS data, contact data, and synchronization payloads) shall be encrypted in transit using a protocol appropriate for the Windows CE and Palm OS platform capabilities.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transmission of sensitive MLS, client, and credential data over cellular and Wi-Fi networks requires encryption to prevent interception.

generated_requirements.text: Authentication credentials and session tokens stored on the device shall be encrypted at rest using the strongest encryption algorithm supported by the target platform (Windows CE or Palm OS), and the system documentation shall explicitly identify and mitigate the risk of operating on these legacy platforms with limited security patch availability.
generated_requirements.rationale: Windows CE and Palm OS are end-of-life or near-end-of-life platforms with limited security patching; credential storage must be hardened to compensate for OS-level vulnerabilities.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based authorization such that each authenticated realtor can access only their own client contacts, appointments, saved searches, and call logs, and cannot access data belonging to other realtors.
generated_requirements.rationale: Data isolation between realtor accounts is required to protect client confidentiality and prevent unauthorized access to proprietary business data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate a CMA report within 60 seconds of the realtor submitting the report criteria, under normal operating conditions, for 100% of report generation requests.
generated_requirements.rationale: The 60-second CMA generation ceiling is a hard performance constraint defined in the business objectives and technical constraints sections.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall function in a degraded read-only offline mode using locally cached data with no dependency on any network connection, and shall automatically detect and transition between online and offline modes without requiring realtor intervention.
generated_requirements.rationale: Seamless offline operation is a core reliability requirement ensuring agents remain productive regardless of connectivity conditions in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from any unplanned application crash or device restart and restore the application to a usable state — including all saved preferences, cached data, and queued offline write operations — without data loss, within 60 seconds of relaunch.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 requires 100% preference restoration after failure; rapid, complete recovery is essential for field agents who cannot tolerate extended downtime.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall be developed and delivered as separate native builds for Windows CE and Palm OS, each conforming to the respective platform's SDK, UI guidelines, and resource constraints, with feature parity between both platform builds for all in-scope capabilities.
generated_requirements.rationale: The technical constraint mandates support for both Windows CE and Palm OS; feature parity ensures all realtors receive equivalent capability regardless of device platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system's backend synchronization and MLS integration components shall be designed with clearly documented, versioned interfaces so that future platform additions or MLS provider changes can be accommodated without requiring changes to the core application business logic.
generated_requirements.rationale: Decoupled, versioned integration interfaces reduce the cost and risk of future platform expansions and MLS provider changes as the organization scales.

generated_requirements.text: The local image cache on the device shall be subject to a defined maximum size limit appropriate to the target device's storage capacity, and the system shall automatically evict the least-recently-used images when the cache limit is reached, prioritising retention of images associated with saved search results.
generated_requirements.rationale: Resource-constrained Palm OS and Windows CE devices require disciplined image cache management to prevent storage exhaustion while preserving the most relevant offline content.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a clearly visible indicator of the current operating mode (online or offline) and, when in offline mode, shall display the timestamp of the most recent successful synchronization for each data domain (contacts, appointments, MLS listings).
generated_requirements.rationale: Agents must always know the currency of the data they are viewing to make informed professional decisions and set accurate client expectations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all authentication attempts (successful and failed), recording the user identifier, timestamp, device identifier, and outcome, and shall lock an account after five consecutive failed authentication attempts, requiring administrator intervention to unlock.
generated_requirements.rationale: Authentication logging and account lockout are standard security controls required to detect and prevent brute-force credential attacks on a system holding sensitive MLS and client data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide the realtor with a single-tap action to initiate driving directions to a property directly from the MLS search result detail view or the map view, without requiring the realtor to navigate to a separate directions screen or re-enter the property address.
generated_requirements.rationale: Minimising navigation steps between the search result, map, and directions features is critical to achieving the no-training usability requirement and reducing field workflow friction.

sme_subject: MLS integration and mobile real estate platform architect
sme_advisory: This system sits at the intersection of MLS data governance, mobile offline-first architecture, and real estate industry compliance — a combination that generalist engineers frequently underestimate. The requirements engineer must pay close attention to MLS board rules (RETS/RESO standards, IDX/VOW policy compliance, and data display obligations) that impose non-negotiable constraints on how listing data may be stored, cached, displayed, and aged out on a mobile device. Additionally, the Windows CE and Palm OS platform targets impose severe resource constraints (memory, storage, CPU, screen resolution) that will directly shape performance, caching strategy, and UI design requirements in ways that modern mobile assumptions will not anticipate.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a recycled parts search function accessible exclusively to users assigned the Collision Estimator role; users with Adjuster or Supervisor Adjuster roles shall not be able to access or invoke this function.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 mandates that only Collision Estimators may search for recycled parts, enforcing role-based access control.

generated_requirements.text: The recycled parts search shall accept vehicle information and damaged parts information as input parameters and shall query the ChoiceParts-integrated parts inventory, filtering results to the estimator's list of preferred suppliers as the primary search scope.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 requires the primary search to be conducted against preferred suppliers using vehicle and parts data.

generated_requirements.text: If the primary preferred-supplier recycled parts search returns zero results, the system shall automatically execute a secondary search expanded to all suppliers within a 30-mile radius of the vehicle's ZIP code and shall clearly indicate to the estimator that the fallback search has been triggered.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 mandates automatic 30-mile radius fallback when no preferred-supplier results are found.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent an estimator from applying recycled parts to a collision estimate when both the primary preferred-supplier search and the 30-mile radius fallback search return no available parts, and shall display an explanatory message to the estimator.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 prohibits applying recycled parts to an estimate when no parts are available from any search.

generated_requirements.text: At the moment an estimator's recycled parts search is executed, the system shall create an immutable snapshot of the exact set of parts results returned by the ChoiceParts feed at that point in time, link this snapshot to the associated estimate record, and store it separately from the live ChoiceParts data so that subsequent feed updates do not alter the snapshot.
generated_requirements.rationale: Estimatics regulations require audit of parts usage to be based on what was available at the time of the estimate, not current inventory.

generated_requirements.text: Each recycled parts search result snapshot shall record the ChoiceParts feed version identifier and the timestamp of the last successful feed synchronization at the time the search was performed, and this metadata shall be included in any audit report generated from that estimate.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regulators require that the audit trail capture the exact dataset version used at search time to ensure auditability of data staleness.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall synchronize the ChoiceParts recycled parts data feed once per day at approximately 1:00 AM, and shall record the timestamp and success or failure status of each synchronization attempt.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-22 specifies the nightly ChoiceParts synchronization schedule, and TC-11 requires the system to accommodate this fixed integration window.

generated_requirements.text: If the ChoiceParts nightly feed synchronization fails or has not completed successfully, the system shall display a prominent data-staleness warning to the estimator before any search is executed, showing the timestamp of the last successful synchronization, and shall require the estimator to explicitly acknowledge the warning before proceeding with a search against stale data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Graceful degradation and disclosure of data staleness is required to maintain regulatory integrity when the ChoiceParts feed fails.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a preferred repair facility search function accessible to users with the Adjuster role, accepting vehicle year, make, and model, vehicle location (street address, city, state, and ZIP code), and a search radius between 1 and 30 miles as input parameters.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 defines the required inputs for the preferred repair facility search.

generated_requirements.text: When a ZIP code is not provided by the adjuster, the system shall automatically resolve the ZIP code from the street address, city, and state using the corporate geocoding or address validation service; if the resolution service is unavailable or returns ambiguous results, the system shall prompt the adjuster to enter the ZIP code manually rather than blocking the workflow or failing silently.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 requires ZIP code resolution from address data, and the domain expert identifies failure-handling for this integration as a common gap.

generated_requirements.text: The preferred repair facility search shall return the repair facility with the highest aggregate rating that matches the vehicle location and radius criteria as the system-recommended preferred facility.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-11 specifies that the highest-rated matching facility is returned as the preferred recommendation.

generated_requirements.text: If no rated repair facility exists within the specified search radius, the system shall return a list of all repair facilities within that radius for the adjuster to select from, rather than returning an error or empty result.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-12 requires a fallback list when no preferred facility can be determined.

generated_requirements.text: The preferred repair facility recommendation screen shall display configurable, jurisdiction-specific disclosure text informing the claimant of their right to choose any repair facility; this disclosure text shall be displayed before or alongside the facility recommendation and shall be configurable per state or jurisdiction by authorized administrators.
generated_requirements.rationale: Anti-steering disclosure is a regulatory requirement in most U.S. states and must be presented at the point of facility recommendation.

generated_requirements.text: The adjuster shall be able to override the system-recommended preferred repair facility on an estimate assignment, and the system shall record the override action, the identity of the adjuster, the original recommendation, and the selected facility in the immutable audit log.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-13 permits adjuster override, and SOX/regulatory requirements mandate that all data modifications be logged with full context.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a recycled parts audit report function accessible exclusively to users with the Adjuster or Supervisor Adjuster role; users with the Collision Estimator role shall not be able to request or view audit reports.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-02 restricts audit report access to the Adjuster role, supporting compliance and governance.

generated_requirements.text: The audit report shall be generated by comparing the immutable recycled parts search result snapshot associated with the estimate against the parts actually applied to the collision estimate, and shall include: (a) total number of recycled parts used in the estimate; (b) total number of available recycled parts from the original search snapshot; (c) the recycled parts utilization percentage, calculated as (recycled parts used ÷ available recycled parts from snapshot) × 100, rounded to two decimal places; (d) individual line-item scores for each available part; and (e) a total audit score summing all line-item scores.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-14 and BR-15 define audit report content, and the domain expert requires precise specification of the utilization percentage formula for regulatory scrutiny.

generated_requirements.text: Each generated audit report shall record the identifier and version number of the scoring algorithm used to produce it, and the system shall maintain a version-controlled repository of all scoring algorithm definitions that can be retrieved and disclosed to regulatory examiners on request.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert requires that the scoring methodology be version-controlled and disclosable to regulators.

generated_requirements.text: Audit reports shall be accessible to any adjuster or supervisor adjuster within the same insurance company, not only the adjuster who originally requested the report, and shall remain accessible for the full data retention period applicable to the estimate's jurisdiction.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-16 requires audit reports to be accessible to other adjusters at later points in time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a repair facility ratings management function accessible to users with the Supervisor Adjuster role, allowing them to view, enter, and save ratings on a scale of 1 to 10 for each repair facility across defined rating categories that include, at minimum, attempted use of recycled parts and actual use of recycled parts.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-03, BR-17, and BR-18 define the ratings management function and its minimum required categories.

generated_requirements.text: When a repair facility has no previously saved ratings, the system shall display a blank ratings entry form rather than an error message or empty-state error condition.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-19 explicitly requires a blank form rather than an error for facilities with no prior ratings.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect concurrent edit conflicts when two or more supervisor adjusters attempt to update ratings for the same repair facility simultaneously; upon detecting a conflict, the system shall notify the second user of the conflict, display the current saved values, and require the second user to explicitly confirm or discard their changes — silent overwrite of ratings data shall not be permitted.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert identifies concurrent edit conflict detection as a frequently missed pattern in ratings management screens.

generated_requirements.text: Every create, update, and delete operation on estimates, repair facility ratings, and audit reports shall be recorded in an immutable audit log that captures: the user's identity, role, and IP address; the timestamp of the operation; the name of the data field modified; the before value; and the after value. No application user role — including Supervisor Adjuster — shall be able to alter or delete audit log entries.
generated_requirements.rationale: SOX Sections 302 and 404 and estimatics regulations require tamper-evident, comprehensive audit logs for all claims-related data modifications.

generated_requirements.text: All actions performed under the Supervisor Adjuster role — particularly repair facility ratings creation and modification — shall be explicitly tagged in the audit log with the role context 'Supervisor Adjuster' in addition to the user identity, to enable regulators and internal auditors to verify that only authorized personnel modified ratings.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert requires role-elevation audit trail entries to support regulatory and internal audit verification of authorized ratings changes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce company-level data isolation such that users authenticated under one insurance company can only access claim records, estimates, audit reports, ratings, and audit logs belonging to that company; this isolation shall be enforced at the database and data-access layer, not solely at the application or UI layer.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 and the domain expert require data isolation at the data layer to satisfy legal discovery obligations and prevent cross-contamination of insurer data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control for all three defined roles — Collision Estimator, Adjuster, and Supervisor Adjuster — ensuring that each role can only access the functions and data explicitly permitted by the business rules, and that role assignments are sourced from the corporate identity and access management system.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 through BR-03 define role-specific permissions that must be enforced consistently across all system functions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all user-entered data against defined field-level rules (type, range, format, and mandatory constraints) before persisting any record, and shall display specific, actionable validation error messages to the user for each invalid field.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-21 requires validation of all entered data to prevent invalid data from being persisted.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide in-system contextual help content covering at least 90% of application screens, accessible without leaving the current screen context.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD KPI and scope section require in-system help on 90% of screens to support learnability in a two-day training model.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support regulatory examination access by allowing authorized internal compliance officers or regulatory examiners — operating through a designated privileged access mechanism that does not require standard application user accounts — to retrieve complete, unaltered audit trails, audit reports, and recycled parts search snapshots for any estimate within the applicable retention period.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert requires that regulatory examiners can access audit data without application-layer user accounts, as mandated by estimatics and insurance regulations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce configurable data retention periods for claim records, audit logs, recycled parts search snapshots, and repair facility ratings, with the minimum retention period set to the longest applicable state insurance record retention requirement (no less than 7 years) and the ability to configure different retention periods per jurisdiction.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce an inactivity session timeout of no more than 10 minutes for all user roles; upon timeout, the session shall be invalidated and the user shall be required to re-authenticate before resuming any activity, to prevent unauthorized access on shared workstations in repair facility environments.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert identifies shared-device repair shop environments as requiring short inactivity timeouts to prevent session hijacking between estimators.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be delivered as a web-based application compatible with Internet Explorer version 5.5 and above and Mozilla Firefox version 1.0 and above, and shall render all functions correctly in both browsers without requiring browser-specific workarounds visible to the user.

generated_requirements.text: The recycled parts search function shall return results to the user within 15 seconds for 95% of search requests under normal operating load, measured from the moment the user submits the search to the moment results are displayed.

generated_requirements.text: The preferred repair facility search function shall return results to the user within 8 seconds for 95% of search requests under normal operating load, measured from search submission to results display.

generated_requirements.text: The audit report generation function shall return the completed report to the requesting user within 10 seconds for 95% of requests under normal operating load, measured from report request submission to report display.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 1,500 simultaneous users at launch without degradation of defined response time SLAs, and shall be architected to scale to support 10,000 concurrent users within two years of launch without requiring a fundamental redesign of the system architecture.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall process and handle a daily throughput of approximately 50,000 recycled parts records from the ChoiceParts feed synchronization without exceeding the nightly maintenance window or degrading daytime query performance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve 98% uptime during scheduled availability windows, measured monthly; unplanned downtime shall not exceed 2% of total scheduled available time in any calendar month.

generated_requirements.text: All planned maintenance activities shall be scheduled and executed exclusively within established corporate maintenance windows; no maintenance that causes user-visible downtime or degraded performance shall be performed outside these windows.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall adhere to corporate Architecture guidelines governing technology stack selection, integration patterns, and deployment architecture, and shall adhere to corporate User Interface Guidelines and corporate color scheme standards in all user-facing screens.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable and upgradeable exclusively by the corporate middleware technology team using documented installation and upgrade procedures; the system shall not require any deployment actions by the application development team in production environments.

generated_requirements.text: The user interface for estimator-facing functions shall be designed for use in physically demanding repair facility environments — accounting for dirty, noisy conditions and limited controlled workspace — with large touch targets, high-contrast display, and minimal required text input to reduce errors and improve usability in non-office settings.

generated_requirements.text: A new user with the Collision Estimator, Adjuster, or Supervisor Adjuster role shall be able to achieve full proficiency in all functions relevant to their role within two days of completing the provided onsite training program.

generated_requirements.text: At least 95% of adjusters and collision estimators surveyed after training and go-live shall rate the system as easy to use, and at least 85% of all users surveyed periodically post-launch shall report overall satisfaction with the product.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall protect all data in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher encryption, and all sensitive data at rest — including claim records, audit logs, recycled parts snapshots, and ratings — shall be encrypted using AES-256 or an equivalent approved corporate encryption standard.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall include protections against denial-of-service attacks and shall be free of known computer viruses and malware; the system shall undergo security vulnerability scanning prior to each production deployment and shall remediate all critical and high-severity findings before go-live.

sme_subject: collision estimatics compliance officer with recycled parts regulatory expertise
sme_advisory: This system sits at the intersection of insurance claims compliance, recycled parts estimatics regulation, and multi-carrier data governance — three areas where generalist requirements engineers routinely under-specify. The requirements engineer must pay particular attention to the immutability and timestamping of audit trail records (SOX and estimatics law both require tamper-evident logs), the precise semantics of the recycled parts utilization percentage calculation in audit reports (regulators and plaintiff attorneys will scrutinize this formula), and the strict company-level data isolation model which must be enforced at the data layer — not just the UI layer — to withstand legal discovery. Additionally, the ChoiceParts integration introduces a 24-hour data staleness window that has direct regulatory implications: if an estimator searches and no parts are found, but parts were available in the prior day's feed, the audit trail must capture the exact dataset version used at search time, not the current feed state.
